matches.keyFactor: Norway illness RESOLVED (team doctor confirmed all players fit — Strand Larsen and Pedersen recovered) and full squad available, only Møller Wolfe (R16 injury) doubtful vs England without Quansah (two-match ban confirmed, also misses SF) and James (hamstring, individual programme, not expected to start) — Haaland's 7 tournament goals now arrives with full Norwegian support against an English back line at its tournament low-point

matches.reading: Kansas City, and the world champions against the tournament's most unexpectedly battered surviving side: Argentina — Messi's eight tournament goals, sole leader of the Golden Boot race after hauling the Albiceleste from two goals down with eleven minutes remaining to a 3-2 stoppage-time win over Egypt, Romero's 79th-minute header and Fernández's added-time winner completing one of the tournament's most breathtaking comebacks — against a Switzerland whose injury list became the most brutal of any remaining side, and who arrive at Arrowhead carrying 120 goalless minutes and a nerve-shredding 4-3 penalty shootout over Colombia as the cost of their passage. Facundo Medina is confirmed as a highly doubtful starter at left-back after a knock sustained against Egypt — Nicolás Tagliafico, international-class cover, provides without structural disruption to Scaloni's shape. Manzambi, whose knee injury ruled him out of the Colombia match, benefits from the additional recovery days Saturday provides; the Nati medical staff report cautious optimism but no clearance. Aebischer and Jaquez remain out with muscle injuries; Switzerland's wide creative threat at its tournament minimum. Scaloni has managed Messi's 39-year-old recovery carefully through the Atlanta aftermath: the world champion's medical team report him ready, and the Golden Boot's leading tally does not diminish. What Xhaka's positional authority cannot substitute across ninety minutes against a world-champion attack in full flight is fresh wide width and late-game attacking variety — and Argentina's vertical press, sharpened by the adversity of the Egypt night, is calibrated to find and punish that structural depletion. Two Argentine goals; the world champions' semi-final place confirmed.
matches.keyFactor: Argentina's Messi (8 tournament goals, Golden Boot leader) and 3-2 Egypt comeback resilience with Medina highly doubtful (Tagliafico covering left-back) vs Switzerland post-120-minute-penalty-exhaustion with Manzambi daily doubt (knee, aided by extra rest), Aebischer and Jaquez also absent — Nati creative width at tournament minimum against the world champions' vertical press

matches.reading: Arlington holds the true final, one round early: the two best sides on earth, the old master Deschamps against the dynasty he can see coming. Mbappé rages gloriously against the dying of the light and scores the goal of the tournament — but Spain pass through the storm as if it were weather for other people. Yamal equalises; Olmo, from the bench, breaks French hearts in the 81st. Pozzo's ghost keeps his record.
matches.keyFactor: Spain's midfield denies France the transitions their whole game is built on

matches.reading: Atlanta, and sixty years of hurt against the champions of everything. The orb shows the tightest match of the tournament: Rice on Messi, Kane against Otamendi, an hour of pure chess. Kane scores; Álvarez answers within four minutes. And then, deep in added time, with penalties looming like an old English nightmare, Messi plays a pass that didn't exist until he imagined it — Lautaro, offside in every universe but this one, wins it. Hurt, renewed.
matches.keyFactor: Argentina's tournament nerve in the moments England have historically failed

matches.reading: Miami hosts the match nobody wants and the neutrals adore. Deschamps' final ninety minutes in the dugout; Tuchel facing his old Bundesliga shadow. The kids play — Doué, Zaïre-Émery, Gordon, Quansah — and Mbappé, chasing the Golden Boot's last goals, scores twice to claim it. Bronze for the old master's farewell; England fly home with familiar lessons.
matches.keyFactor: Mbappé's Golden Boot hunt gives the dead rubber a living pulse

matches.venue: MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford (New York/New Jersey)
matches.reading: MetLife, July 19. The dynasty against the dying of the greatest light: Spain's eternal midfield against Messi's final game for the only prize he could still want twice. The orb has never glowed brighter — Messi scores, of course he scores, a first-half free kick that makes time stop. But the future is patient: Yamal levels before the hour with the arrogance of seventeen... eighteen... whatever age genius is now, and Pedri's winner in the 74th is four hundred passes old. Spain, champions of the world — the second star, the dynasty confirmed, in the city where the orb first foresaw it.
matches.keyFactor: The best midfield on earth against the best story — and midfields win World Cups
